items.title: Sam Neill Inspired a Generation of Scientists
items.summary: The actor, who died at age 78 on Monday, embodied a passionate approach to research and positive masculinity in his role as paleontologist Alan Grant in the 1993 blockbuster Jurassic Park.

items.title: Siri AI Is Becoming Apple’s Everything Tool
items.summary: Apple’s revamped Siri is more than a voice assistant; it’s now the backbone of the iPhone user experience. You can try it now through the iOS 27 public beta.

items.title: The Problem With VAR at the 2026 World Cup Isn’t the Technology—It’s Who Interprets It
items.summary: The video assistant referee system, or VAR, has led to some controversial calls at the 2026 World Cup. Here’s why.

items.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
items.summary: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell proprietary models are somehow acting like Trojan horses.

items.title: Apple says former employee exploited ‘rare’ bug to download confidential files after leaving for OpenAI
items.summary: Apple would not comment on the "security breach," which allegedly allowed a former employee to download sensitive files from Apple's network long after he departed the company for rival OpenAI.

items.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
items.summary: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring Apple hardware to interviews. Here are the complaint’s most eye-catching claims.

items.title: Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to run to safety
items.summary: Malcolm Timbrell's wife and their friends are thought to have died when a devastating wildfire tore through their village in Spain.

items.title: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office
items.summary: Tamás Sulyok was widely seen as a loyalist of former prime minister Viktor Orbán, who lost power in April after 16 years.

items.title: A July rate hike from the Fed? The odds are rising
items.summary: Chances of a rate hike in July by the Federal Reserve rose as oil prices jumped on the latest developments in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.summary: The Fed governor said inflation has expanded beyond the often-cited drivers such as the energy price spike in tariffs.

items.summary: Big banks are set to report booming Q2 revenue as the SpaceX IPO, Iran war volatility and a rebound in commercial lending fuel Wall Street's "sweet spot."

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Physicists say quantum mechanics may not need imaginary numbers after all
items.summary: Physicists from Heinrich Heine University Düsseldorf (HHU) have examined a fundamental property of quantum mechanics in collaboration with the German Aerospace Center (DLR). In the scientific journal Physical Review Letters, they show that this theory does not necessarily need to be formulated with imaginary numbers – real numbers can in fact also be used. The American Physical Society has also dedicated a “Highlight” to these findings in its Physics Magazine.

items.title: Losing just 80 minutes of sleep a night could make you gain weight
items.summary: Sleeping about an hour and 20 minutes less each night for six weeks caused participants to gain weight and spend more time inactive. Researchers found that even mild, realistic sleep loss, similar to what many adults experience, had measurable effects. They warn that if this pattern continues over months or years, the health consequences could become much more significant, including a higher risk of diabetes and heart disease.

items.title: Stephen Hawking's black hole laws just got a major upgrade
items.summary: Scientists have developed a new framework that could finally apply the laws of thermodynamics to real, ever-changing black holes instead of only perfectly stable ones. The advance may improve our understanding of black hole mergers, evaporation, and the powerful gravitational wave events detected by observatories like LIGO.

items.title: Whether lefty or righty, practice makes the difference
items.summary: Researchers looking for the origins of left-right dominance in the brain found no innately better motor skills on either side.

items.title: Meet the Milky Way’s puffiest planets
items.summary: Two “superpuff” planets orbiting a sunlike star over 1,000 light-years from Earth are as big as Jupiter and as dense as cotton candy.

items.title: Pluto has landslides
items.summary: New Horizons data reveal Pluto’s first six confirmed landslides along steep crater rims.
